title: Is abortion permissible for a pregnancy that occurred outside of marriage?
summary: Abortion before 120 days (4 months) is permissible in cases of pregnancy outside of marriage, as the ruh (soul) has not yet been blown in. After 120 days, it is generally not allowed.

title: Are stock options halal in Islam?
summary: Stock options are haram (impermissible) according to the vast majority of Islamic finance bodies including the OIC Fiqh Academy, European Fatwa Council, and AAOIFI, due to excessive gharar (uncertainty) and qimar (gambling).

title: Is Bitcoin and cryptocurrency halal or haram in Islam?
summary: Yasir Qadhi holds that Bitcoin and established cryptocurrencies are permissible (halal) based on the default Islamic legal principle that all transactions are permissible unless proven otherwise. He a

title: What are the Islamic rulings regarding a child born out of wedlock?
summary: Yasir Qadhi provides a comprehensive ruling on children born out of wedlock: the child is absolutely blameless and deserves full love, dignity, and respect regardless of the circumstances of their bir
tags: does illegitimate child inherit from father

title: Does a civil divorce filed by the wife count as an Islamic divorce?
summary: When a husband files for civil divorce and signs the paperwork, it counts as an Islamic talaq because writing a divorce is the same as verbalizing it. However, when a wife files for civil divorce, the
tags: does civil divorce count as Islamic divorce

title: Is taking a conventional mortgage from a bank considered the prohibited riba?
summary: The riba in modern mortgages is a secondary type prohibited by hadith, not the primary Quranic riba. Major fatwa councils permit conventional mortgages under conditions of genuine need such as buying a home.

title: Is cosmetic surgery permissible to correct a birth defect or for beautification?
summary: Corrective surgery to fix a birth defect, acquired disfigurement, or abnormality is permissible by near-unanimous scholarly consensus, based on the Islamic maxim that harm should be eliminated. Howeve
tags: is cosmetic surgery halal in islam

title: To what extent can a divorced woman advertise that she is looking for a husband?
summary: It is permissible for a divorced woman to make it known in a dignified manner that she is seeking a husband, including through word of mouth and Islamic marriage websites.

title: Is it permissible to drink water while standing?
summary: It is better to sit when drinking water, but drinking while standing is permissible. Ibn Abbas reported seeing the Prophet drink while standing. When circumstances make standing natural, there is no issue.

title: Is saying "Oh my God" in frustration to parents equivalent to the prohibited "uff"?
summary: The prohibition of saying "uff" is about expressing frustration and contempt toward parents, not the specific word. Any expression said with a tone of frustration or contempt carries the same prohibition.

title: What should you do about halal meat standards when visiting someone else's home?
summary: If you follow a stricter halal meat opinion, you may politely ask about your host's standards without offending them. Participate in the gathering and eat vegetarian if needed. Stunning is makruh but does not make the animal haram.

title: Is house, car, and life insurance permitted in Islam?
summary: Car and health insurance are permitted due to necessity in Western countries. House and property insurance are also allowed. Life insurance remains impermissible unless provided automatically by an employer.

title: Can Muslims invest in stocks and the S&P 500?
summary: Investing in stocks is permissible in Islam if certain conditions are met. The company's primary product must be halal, and the proportion of interest-based loans and liquid assets must fall within ac
tags: is stock trading halal or haram

title: Is it appropriate for a husband to joke about taking a second wife?
summary: The Prophet (peace be upon him) never joked about taking a second wife, and doing so is not from the Sunnah. A woman has feelings, and it is not gentlemanly for a husband to provoke those feelings by

title: Can a wife leave the house and go to her parents during the iddah period after talaq?
summary: After a husband gives one talaq, the wife should remain in the same house for the three-month iddah period (three menstrual cycles) as a safeguard Allah has placed to protect the marriage. Leaving the
tags: can wife leave house after talaq
where should wife stay during iddah

title: Is it permissible to remove a family member from life support or a ventilator?
summary: Medical treatment including ventilators is mubah (permissible) but not wajib (obligatory). Removing life support from someone in a vegetative state with no chance of recovery is not considered murder.

title: How do I make up for years of missed salah after becoming practicing?
summary: There are two overlapping scholarly opinions on making up years of missed salah. The majority opinion holds that one should make up the missed prayers (qada) by praying extra over the same number of y
tags: how to make up missed prayers in Islam
do I have to make up years of missed salah
can you make up missed prayers in Islam

title: Is it allowed for a husband to force his wife into intimacy in Islam?
summary: A husband does not have the right to force himself upon his wife in Islam. While earlier scholars may have allowed this based on their cultural context and ijtihad, this opinion is not binding because
tags: can a husband force his wife in Islam

title: Does Islamic law dictate specific household chores and roles between husband and wife?
summary: Islamic law does not dictate specific chore assignments. The Quranic principle is ma'ruf (treating each other with kindness normal for their time and culture), with arbitration and divorce as escalating steps for disputes.

title: How does a married daughter balance obligations between her husband and her parents?
summary: A married woman's primary obligation shifts to her husband, but her relationship with parents never ceases. The husband must be fair and give her reasonable opportunity to care for her parents.

title: Can you wipe over regular (non-leather) socks during wudu?
summary: It is permissible to wipe over regular cloth socks during wudu; they do not need to be leather. The conditions are that socks must cover the ankles and you must have had wudu before putting them on.

title: Can you break your salah for a medical emergency in the masjid?
summary: It is not only permissible but obligatory to break one's salah to attend to a medical emergency. If someone faints or has a medical emergency during congregational prayer, the one or two people neares
tags: can you break prayer for emergency
someone faints in masjid during salah
is it allowed to break salah

title: Is wearing a necklace, earrings, or jewelry permissible for men in Islam?
summary: Gold and silk are categorically forbidden for men by explicit prophetic prohibition — this applies to gold chains, rings, necklaces, and any silk clothing regardless of whether one is alone or in publ
tags: can men wear necklaces in islam
is it haram for men to wear earrings
men wearing chains halal or haram
islamic ruling on men wearing jewelry
can muslim men wear silver chains

title: Is leaving a negative review for a Muslim-owned restaurant considered backbiting?
summary: A negative factual review of a Muslim-owned restaurant is not backbiting. Business reviews are a public service, not the exposure of a private sin.

title: Is obedience to parents unconditionally obligatory in Islam?
summary: Islam commands good conduct toward parents, not unconditional obedience. Obedience is obligatory only when the command is Islamically good, directly benefits the parents, and does not cause disproportionate hardship. You must never be rude regardless.

title: Am I obliged to remind a parent suffering from dementia to pray?
summary: When a person has dementia, the pen is lifted from them (no longer held accountable). Reminding them is good but not obligatory. In advanced stages, they lack the cognitive capacity required.

title: Is it permissible for Muslims to celebrate religious holidays of other faiths like Christmas or Diwali?
summary: Muslims do not celebrate religious festivals of other faiths. However, Muslim converts may attend family gatherings on Christmas to maintain family ties, as long as they avoid religious rituals.

title: Is it permissible for Muslims to celebrate Halloween?
summary: Halloween should be avoided due to lingering associations with demons and the supernatural, but it does not constitute shirk or kufr. Muslims who participate out of ignorance should be treated with empathy.

title: Is wearing the hijab obligatory in Islam and what is the wisdom behind it?
summary: The hijab (head covering and loose body covering) is obligatory for Muslim women by unanimous consensus of all schools of Islamic law—Sunni and beyond. The Quran explicitly commands this in Surah al-N
tags: can you take hijab off for safety
what does quran say about hijab

title: What constitutes impermissible imitation of non-Muslims (tashabbuh)?
summary: Prohibited imitation means deliberately adopting something that characterizes a civilization of kufr foreign to one's own. For Western Muslims, speaking English and wearing Western clothes is not imitation.

title: Is the mehndi (henna) ceremony before weddings permissible despite its Hindu origins?
summary: The mehndi ceremony is permissible despite its Hindu origins because the religious connotation has been completely lost. It is now a purely cultural practice among Muslims.

title: Does a practice having pagan origins make it haram for Muslims?
summary: Pagan origins alone do not make a practice haram. What matters is the current cultural association, not the historical origin. If pagan connotation is gone, the practice is permissible.

title: Are personal private celebrations like birthdays, graduations, and promotions permissible in Islam?
summary: Personal, private, non-religious celebrations are fully permissible. The Sharia is silent about them, and making something annual (like birthdays) does not make it bid'ah.

title: Is it permissible to have a secret marriage in Islam?
summary: A secret marriage may be technically valid if basic conditions are met (wali, witnesses, mahr), but it is strongly discouraged. No marriage will flourish if done in secret.

title: Is it permissible to participate in secular national holidays like the 4th of July?
summary: Secular communal celebrations like the 4th of July are cultural matters, not religious rituals. Individual participation through family gatherings, barbecues, and fireworks is permissible.

title: Is it permissible for Muslims to celebrate Thanksgiving?
summary: Thanksgiving is a secular, non-religious holiday with no ritual of deity worship. It is more neutral than Halloween and permissible for Muslims to participate in.

title: Is yoga permissible for Muslims?
summary: Yoga is permissible in the West where it is practiced purely as exercise. However, it should be avoided by Muslims in India where it retains strong Hindu religious associations.

title: If a husband and wife separate for years without a formal divorce, does the marriage still exist?
summary: Separation alone, even for years, does not constitute a divorce. If no talaq was uttered or filed, the marriage remains valid and can be resumed without a new nikah.

title: Is it permissible to short sell stocks in Islam?
summary: Short selling stocks is not permissible according to the vast majority of Islamic finance councils, primarily because you are selling what you do not own, which contradicts the Prophet's explicit hadith.

title: Can you shorten the prayer (qasr) when traveling for pleasure or vacation?
summary: By unanimous scholarly consensus, any permissible (mubah) travel qualifies for shortening prayer. Vacation and leisure trips are included — qasr is not limited to business travel.

title: Should the hijab be enforced by an Islamic government?
summary: Yasir Qadhi affirms that hijab is a clear religious obligation in Islam according to scholarly consensus, but argues that enforcing it through government coercion is counterproductive and not aligned

title: Can you combine the six fasts of Shawwal with making up missed Ramadan fasts?
summary: You cannot combine the six fasts of Shawwal with makeup Ramadan fasts under a dual intention. You may fast Mondays/Thursdays for makeup and get the sunnah reward. It is better to finish Ramadan fasts before Shawwal fasts, but not required.

title: Are student loans permissible in Islam?
summary: Student loans involving interest are riba and thus haram by default. However, the European Fiqh Council, AMJA, and the Fiqh Council of North America all permit students to take such loans when no alte
tags: is it haram to take student loans

title: Is there one correct 'Sunnah' way to pray salah?
summary: There is no single 'correct' way to pray that renders all other ways invalid. All four established Sunni schools of law (Hanafi, Shafi'i, Maliki, Hanbali) derive their prayer methods from authentic Su
tags: where to place hands in prayer
which madhab is correct for salah

reading quran from phone in salah
can I hold mushaf in prayer

title: Does saying triple talaq in one sitting count as three divorces or one?
summary: The correct opinion, which Yasir Qadhi personally holds, is that a triple talaq given in one sitting constitutes only one talaq — not three. This was the position of Ibn Taymiyyah, who was famously ja
tags: does triple talaq count as one
saying talaq three times in anger
is triple talaq one or three

title: Is vanilla extract permissible in food despite containing alcohol?
summary: Vanilla extract in food is permissible. No food product flavored with vanilla can cause intoxication, so it does not fall under the prophetic prohibition. The same applies to nutmeg and similar extracts.

title: Can a woman travel for Hajj without a mahram?
summary: Yasir Qadhi holds that a woman may travel for Hajj (and other permissible or virtuous travel) without a mahram if the environment is generally safe, following the position of the Shafi'i school, Ibn T
tags: can a woman travel without mahram

title: Is it permissible for women to hold leadership roles and board positions in the masjid?
summary: Nothing in the Sharia forbids women from serving on a masjid board or holding leadership positions. Restricting women from permissible roles risks pushing them away from the community.

title: Is it haram to work for a company that has business ties to Israel?
summary: Yasir Qadhi differentiates between companies directly aiding and abetting genocide — such as those manufacturing weapons or building factories on occupied land — and generic corporations that merely d
tags: is my job haram if company does business with Israel

title: Is zakat due on a woman's gold jewelry?
summary: The safer position is that zakat is due on women's gold jewelry at 2.5%, even though the majority of scholars say it is not required on gold used for personal adornment. The Hanafi position, which requires zakat, has strong evidence since gold can function as currency.

title: How do you calculate zakat on long-term investments like 401k and mutual funds?
summary: For long-term investments not actively traded, zakat is due on one-third of the total value (2.5% of one-third). For day traders actively buying and selling, zakat is due on the full 100%.
